name: Google Drive API
what_it_does: Google's file storage and synchronization API for creating, reading, organizing, and sharing files and Google Workspace documents (Docs, Sheets, Slides) programmatically.
use_cases: Reading and writing files to Google Drive from agents
Creating and updating Google Docs/Sheets/Slides programmatically
Organizing files and folders in shared drives
Exporting Google Workspace documents to standard formats (PDF, docx)
Monitoring Drive for file changes via watch/push notifications
not_for: High-throughput binary file storage (S3 is more efficient)
Teams that don't use Google Workspace
best_when: Your team is in the Google Workspace ecosystem and you need to automate document creation, organization, or retrieval from agents.
avoid_when: You need pure binary file storage at scale, or your team is not on Google Workspace.

auth.notes: OAuth 2.0 for user-context. Service Account for server-to-server access. Domain-wide delegation required for impersonating workspace users. Many granular scopes.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free for personal (15GB storage); Google Workspace billing separate
pricing.paid_tiers: Part of Google Cloud/Workspace billing. API calls are free within quota.
pricing.notes: API itself is free within daily quota limits. Google Workspace business subscriptions have their own pricing.

performance.rate_limits: 1000 requests/100 seconds per user; 10 requests/second per user

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Google-standard error format with code, message, and errors array. Quota errors well-identified.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: No idempotency keys. Resumable upload sessions can be resumed but not deduplicated.
known_agent_gotchas: Service accounts cannot access user Drive files without domain-wide delegation explicitly configured
Google Workspace Docs/Sheets use a proprietary MIME type - must export to get standard file formats
Per-user rate limits (not per-app) make it easy to hit limits with automated agents
OAuth scopes are very granular - requesting too broad a scope triggers user consent friction
Shared drives and My Drive have different permission models and API parameters

security_score_components.security_notes: OAuth2 with granular drive scopes (drive.readonly, drive.file, drive.appdata). Service accounts with domain delegation for automated access. SOC2, ISO27001, FedRAMP. Google Workspace audit logs available.
